name: Namaka
description: Namaka, also known as Nā-maka-o-Kahaʻi, is a significant sea goddess in Hawaiian mythology. She is the elder sister of Pele, the fire goddess, and the daughter of Haumea and Kane. Namaka is primarily associated with the ocean, controlling its waves, currents, and tides. She is often depicted as a powerful and sometimes jealous figure, known for her clashes with her sister, Pele. These conflicts are often attributed to Pele's volcanic activity encroaching upon Namaka's oceanic domain. Namaka's powers extend to controlling sea creatures and influencing weather patterns at sea. Her role in the creation myths is less pronounced than Pele's, but her influence over the marine environment is undeniable, making her a respected and feared deity by those who depend on the ocean's bounty. The battles between Namaka and Pele are not merely sibling rivalry but represent the ongoing tension between the forces of water and fire, shaping the Hawaiian landscape. Namaka's control over the seas allowed her to protect or punish those who dared to venture into her realm. She embodies the untamed power and vastness of the ocean.
subtitle: Sea Goddess, Sister of Pele

keyMyths.title: The Conflict with Pele
keyMyths.description: Namaka's jealousy of Pele's volcanic power led to battles between them, resulting in Pele being driven from various islands. These conflicts symbolize the struggle between fire and water in shaping the Hawaiian Islands.

keyMyths.title: Namaka's Control of the Sea
keyMyths.description: Namaka is said to have dominion over the ocean's currents and waves, able to summon storms and protect sailors. Her power is a constant reminder of the sea's unpredictable nature.

keyMyths.title: The Birth of the Islands
keyMyths.description: While Pele is credited with creating the islands through volcanic activity, Namaka's waves and currents continue to shape their coastlines. This illustrates the complementary roles of the sisters in the islands' formation.

sources.author: Martha Warren Beckwith
sources.description: A comprehensive study of Hawaiian myths and legends.
sources.text: A comprehensive study of Hawaiian myths and legends.
